assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item exposed to foreign exchange risk in the consolidated financial statements.
The question asks about possible hedge-accounting model eligibility rather than measurement of the dividend itself.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes recognised assets or liabilities as possible hedged items, which makes the fair value hedge model a relevant top-level hedge-accounting approach for a recognised foreign-currency monetary receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also treats exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items as a distinct hedge-accounting model, so it is a peer top-level approach to assess for foreign-exchange risk on a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify a separate hedge-accounting model for foreign-exchange exposures relating to a net investment in a foreign operation, which is the other relevant canonical IFRS hedge model in consolidated financial statements.
